Interior-Point Solvers (QOCO and Clarabel)

On this page

  • The QP formulation

  • The Coulomb friction cone

  • KKT conditions

  • QOCO vs Clarabel

  • QOCO backend selection

  • Limitations

  • Config keys

Both QOCO and Clarabel formulate the contact and constraint problem as a convex second-order cone program (SOCP) and solve it with a primal-dual interior-point method. Unlike the iterative projection solvers (PJ, PGS), they enforce contact feasibility as a mathematical constraint rather than a post-projection step, which means they can satisfy the Coulomb friction cone exactly.

The QP formulation

The Moreau step (velocity \(x = (u,\,\Lambda_g,\,\Lambda_\gamma)\), scaled multipliers as defined in Projected Gauss-Seidel) is cast as a convex quadratic program:

\[\min_{x}\; \tfrac{1}{2}\,x^\top P\,x + c^\top x\]

subject to equality constraints from the compliant force laws and cone constraints from contact.

Objective (\(P\), \(c\))

\[\begin{split}P = \begin{pmatrix} M_\mathrm{eff} & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & \tfrac{C}{\theta\,dt^2} & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & \tfrac{A}{\theta\,dt} \end{pmatrix}, \qquad c = -\begin{pmatrix} M u_n + dt\,f^\mathrm{ext} \\[2pt] 0 \\ 0 \end{pmatrix}\end{split}\]

Constraint equations (\(A\), \(b\)) – spring and damper rows

\[\begin{split}A = \begin{pmatrix} W_g^\top & -\tfrac{C}{\theta\,dt^2} & 0 \\[2pt] W_\gamma^\top & 0 & -\tfrac{A}{\theta\,dt} \end{pmatrix}, \qquad b = \begin{pmatrix} -\tfrac{C\,\Lambda_{g,n}}{\theta\,dt^2} - \tfrac{1-\theta}{\theta}W_g^\top u_n - \tfrac{1}{\theta}v_g \\[4pt] - \tfrac{1-\theta}{\theta}W_\gamma^\top u_n - \tfrac{1}{\theta}v_\gamma \end{pmatrix}\end{split}\]

These enforce the compliant constraint laws \(Ax = b\) as hard equalities (hard constraints simply have \(C = 0\) or \(A = 0\), making those rows purely kinematic).

The Coulomb friction cone

For a single frictional contact \(i\) with friction coefficient \(\mu_i\), the admissible impulse set is the scaled second-order cone:

\[K_{\mu_i} = \bigl\{\,\Lambda_{NT} \in \mathbb{R}^3 \;\big|\; \|\Lambda_{T}\| \leq \mu_i\,\Lambda_{N}\,\bigr\}\]

Enforcing \(\Lambda_{NT} \in K_{\mu_i}\) directly requires a scaled cone, which complicates the interior-point system. Following De Saxce and Acary, the implementation introduces the per-contact scaling matrix:

\[S_{\mu_i} = \mathrm{diag}\!\left(\tfrac{1}{\mu_i},\, 1,\, 1\right)\]

and the change of variables \(z_i = S_{\mu_i}^{-1}\Lambda_{NT, i}\), so that the scaled impulse \(z_i\) lives in the standard SOC \(L\):

\[\Lambda_{NT,i} \in K_{\mu_i} \quad\Longleftrightarrow\quad z_i = S_{\mu_i}^{-1}\Lambda_{NT, i} \in L\]

On the velocity side the contact velocity \(\gamma_i = W_{NT,i}^\top u\) is corrected by the De Saxce term \(\bar{\gamma}_i\) which is approximated from velocity of last timestep as \(\bar{\gamma} = \left( \mu\|\gamma_T\|, 0, 0 \right)^\top\).

This allows the frictional contact condition to be written as the standard cone complementarity \(L \ni s_i \perp z_i \in L\). Frictionless contacts reduce to a single row in the non-negative orthant.

Contact friction

The contact friction conditions are expressed as the standard cone complementarity \(h_i - G_i\,x =s_i \in L\) for each frictional contact \(i\).

\[G = \begin{pmatrix} -S_{\mu}^\top W_{NT}^\top & 0 & 0 \end{pmatrix} \qquad h = S_{\mu}^\top\!\left(\gamma^\mathrm{ext} + \bar{\gamma}\right)\]

where \(\gamma^\mathrm{ext}\) is the contact velocity contribution from non-physical moving bodies.

Because the non-convex coulomb friction problem is solved as a convexified problem, the interior-point method does not solve the exact coloumb friction problem.

KKT conditions

The optimal primal-dual solution \((x^*,\, y^*,\, s^*,\, z^*)\) satisfies:

\[\begin{split}P\,x + c + A^\top y + G^\top z &= 0 \quad\text{(stationarity)} \\ A\,x &= b \quad\text{(primal: constraint equations)} \\ h - G\,x &= s \quad\text{(primal: cone slack)} \\ L \ni s^i &\perp z^i \in L \quad \forall\, i \quad\text{(complementarity)}\end{split}\]

Expanding the stationarity condition with the concrete matrices gives:

\[\begin{split}M_\mathrm{eff}\,u + W_g\,y_g + W_\gamma\,y_\gamma - W_{NT} S_\mu\, z &= M u_n + dt\,f^\mathrm{ext} \\ \tfrac{C}{\theta\,dt^2}\,\Lambda_g - \tfrac{C}{\theta\,dt^2}\,y_g &= 0 \;\Rightarrow\; y_g = \Lambda_g \\ \tfrac{A}{\theta\,dt}\,\Lambda_\gamma - \tfrac{A}{\theta\,dt}\,y_\gamma &= 0 \;\Rightarrow\; y_\gamma = \Lambda_\gamma\end{split}\]

So the dual variables \(y_g\) and \(y_\gamma\) coincide with the primal multipliers, and the first KKT equation recovers the discrete momentum balance. The constraint rows enforce the compliant force laws, and the complementarity condition enforces Signorini + Coulomb.

QOCO vs Clarabel

Both backends solve the same physical problem with the same matrices. The differences are in standard form, implementation, and build requirements.

QOCO

Clarabel

Standard form

\(\min \tfrac{1}{2}x^\top Px + c^\top x\) s.t. \(Ax=b\), \(Gx \in \mathcal{K}\)

\(\min \tfrac{1}{2}x^\top Px + q^\top x\) s.t. \(Ax \in \mathcal{K}\)

Equality handling

Separate \(A\), \(b\) matrices

Zero-cone rows prepended to \(A\)

Iterative refinement

Not supported

solver.iter_ref_iters refinement passes per KKT solve

Algorithm

Primal-dual interior point (homogeneous embedding)

Homogeneous self-dual interior point

Warmstart

None (restarts from central path each step)

None

QOCO backend selection

QOCO resolves its backend library at runtime:

  • qoco.backend = auto – tries CUDA first, falls back to CPU

  • qoco.backend = cpu – CPU only

  • qoco.backend = cuda – CUDA only

Limitations

  • No warmstart. The last result might not be feasible in the next timestep, so it cannot directly be used as warmstart.

  • Rebuilt every step. When contacts appear or disappear the sparsity pattern of \(G\) changes, requiring a full solver re-setup.

  • No implicit gyroscopy (moreau.implicit_gyroscopy) and no lambda-theta integration (moreau.lambda_theta). These settings are silently ignored; use PJ or PGS if you need them.

Config keys

Key

Effect

solver.type = qoco / clarabel

Select backend

qoco.backend

auto / cpu / cuda (QOCO only)

solver.max_iterations

Maximum interior-point iterations

solver.tol_abs, solver.tol_rel

Primal/dual feasibility and gap tolerances

solver.kkt_static_reg

Static KKT regularisation constant (both)

solver.kkt_dynamic_reg

Dynamic KKT regularisation delta (both)

solver.iter_ref_iters

Iterative refinement passes per KKT solve (Clarabel only)
